---
title: "GreyVsRed"
output: html_document
---

```{r, include=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(giscoR)
library(sf)

red <- read.csv("records-2026-06-16/records-2026-06-16.csv") |> 
  rename(Longitude = Longitude..WGS84., Latitude = Latitude..WGS84.)
grey <- read.csv("records-2026-06-16-Sciurus-carolinensis/records-2026-06-16-Sciurus-carolinensis.csv") |> 
  rename(Longitude = Longitude..WGS84., Latitude = Latitude..WGS84.)
```

```{r, include=FALSE}
uk <- gisco_get_countries(country="UK")

# 1. Project the UK layer
uk_projected <- st_transform(uk, 27700)

# 2. Get the bounding box area of the UK
bbox <- st_bbox(uk_projected)
bbox_area <- (bbox$xmax - bbox$xmin) * (bbox$ymax - bbox$ymin)

# 3. Calculate the target size for ~100 hexagons
# The area of a regular hexagon is approximately 0.866 * d^2 (where d is cellsize diameter)
target_hex_area <- bbox_area / 1000
hex_cellsize <- sqrt(target_hex_area / 0.866)

# 4. Create the hex grid
hex_grid <- st_make_grid(uk_projected, cellsize = hex_cellsize, square = FALSE) %>%
  st_sf() %>%
  mutate(region_id = row_number())

# 5. Clip it cleanly to the UK coastline
uk_hex_grid <- st_intersection(hex_grid, st_union(uk_projected))

create_density <- function(points) {
  # 1. Project your points
  points_sf <- st_as_sf(points, coords = c("Longitude", "Latitude"), crs = 4326) %>%
    st_transform(27700)
  
  # 2. Spatial Join
  points_in_hex <- st_join(points_sf, uk_hex_grid, join = st_intersects)
  
  # 3. Count points per hexagon
  hex_counts <- points_in_hex %>%
    st_drop_geometry() %>%
    group_by(region_id) %>%
    summarise(point_count = n())
  
  # 4. Join counts back to the hexagonal map shapes
  uk_hex_grid %>%
    left_join(hex_counts, by = "region_id") %>%
    mutate(point_count = replace_na(point_count, 0))
}
```

```{r, echo=FALSE}
graph_uk_density <- function(year) {
  combined_density <- bind_rows(
    mutate(create_density(red |> filter(Year==year)), Squirrel = "Red Squirrels"),
    mutate(create_density(grey |> filter(Year==year)), Squirrel = "Grey Squirrels")
  )
  
  ggplot() +
    geom_sf(data=uk_projected, color = "grey100") +
    geom_sf(data=combined_density, aes(fill = point_count, alpha = point_count), colour="white") +
    facet_wrap(~Squirrel) +
    scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "viridis", name = "Sightings") +
    scale_alpha_continuous(range = c(0, 2.5), guide = "none") +
    theme_minimal() +
    labs(title = sprintf("Sightings of Red vs Grey Squirrels in %d", year),
         caption = gisco_attributions()) +
    theme(axis.text.x=element_blank(), 
          axis.title.x=element_blank(),
          axis.text.y=element_blank(), 
          axis.title.y=element_blank(),
          panel.grid.major = element_blank())
}

graph_uk_density(2020)
```

```{r, echo=FALSE}
sightings <- bind_rows(
    mutate(red |> group_by(Year) |> summarise(n = n()) |> arrange(desc(n)), Squirrel = "Red"),
    mutate(grey |> group_by(Year) |> summarise(n = n()) |> arrange(desc(n)), Squirrel = "Grey")
  ) |> 
  pivot_wider(names_from = Squirrel, values_from = c(n), values_fill = 0) |>
  mutate(Total = Red + Grey, Prop = Red / Total) |>
  filter(Total > 50)

ggplot(sightings, aes(x = Year, y = Prop)) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point(size = 1) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::label_percent(), limits = c(0, NA)) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "The Decline of the Red Squirrel Share in the UK",
    subtitle = "Proportion of total annual squirrel sightings that were Red",
    y = "Share of Total Sightings",
    x = "Year"
  )
```

```{r, include=FALSE}
model <- glm(Red ~ Year + offset(log(Total)), data=sightings, family=poisson)
summary(model)
```
